sglang.title: [Fix] Fix errors when using the device except cuda.
sglang.author: cboss6
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Recent code modifications use cuda stream by default, and result in errors when using device except CUDA. So this PR modified it to use a general way to create and get device stream.

sglang.title: Auto-detect device if not specified in server arguments.
sglang.author: vshekhawat-hlab
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently, the device argument in ServerArgs defaults to "cuda", which forces the use of CUDA even when other hardware accelerators (like XPU, HPU, or CPU) may be available or preferred. This PR aims to make the device selection more dynamic and flexible by automatically detecting the ava

vllm.title: [VLM] Limit multimodal input cache by memory
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Based on the discussion on https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07QP347J4D/p1740687585902709, it makes more sense to limit the input cache based on the memory usage rather than the number of multimodal items.
To enable this, I adopted the LRUCache implementation from cachetools which lets us customi

vllm.title: [Core] Expose API endpoint `/is_sleeping`
vllm.author: waltforme
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR exposes a read-only API to check whether the engine is sleeping. More details are documented as #14311 .
FIX #14311

vllm.title: [VLM] Merged multi-modal processor for Pixtral
vllm.author: Flechman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR aims at implementing the merged multi-modal processor for Pixtral as an effort to contribute to the V1 re-arch for multi-modal models.
Additional changes (by @DarkLight1337 ):

SImplify mask construction for Pixtral-HF
Update type annotation for flatten_2d_lists to avoid unnecessary iteratio

vllm.title: [Kernel] Add more tuned configs
vllm.author: simon-mo
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Supercedes #13919, this PR adds Triton configs from SGLang repo for NVIDIA GPUs that vLLM is missing such as A800, H20, etc. Sincerely thank all the contributors for the open source community.

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix torch distributed stateless PG backend init
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
ProcessGroup.Options was removed in torch 2.5: pytorch/pytorch#132931
This affects data parallel. There was also a bug where the test script would exit successfully even if the subprocs fail, which is why we missed this originally.
